note:
MissingLoginSessionDetector increment 2 — boot-wiring. Wires the increment-1 pure core into the live server behind the developmentAgent dark-feature gate (enabled OMITTED -> resolveDevAgentGate; dark on the fleet, live on a dev agent) and dryRun-first (ConfigDefaults missingLoginSession: { dryRun: true }; raises nothing until a deliberate dryRun:false). A new factory module (missingLoginSessionWiring.ts) adapts the neutral MissingLoginAttention shape into the real TelegramAdapter attention-item input and builds the injected deps from the real subscriptionPool.list() (accounts flagged identityDrift.repairState==='owner-relogin-required' OR actualAccountId==='missing-local-login' -> missing-login accounts) and sessionManager.listRunningSessions() (each running session's login slot/configHome resolved from its subscriptionAccountId via a pool lookup; an unresolvable session is SKIPPED, never guessed, so no false correlation). The detector is constructed ONLY when the gate resolves enabled, registers a synchronous GuardRegistry runtime getter (enabled/dryRun + real lastTickAt liveness), and rides the existing 30s peerPresenceTick fail-toward-silence. New read route GET /pool/missing-login (503 when dark, 200 status() when live). Guard reclassified NOT_A_GUARD -> GUARD_MANIFEST (config, expectRuntime:true, not loadBearing). Migration parity: strip-migration for a default-shaped enabled:false + DEV_GATED_FEATURES entry + CapabilityIndex entry + type + ConfigDefaults default. All three test tiers added (unit factory dep-mapping, integration route dark/live, e2e feature-alive). Additive, signal-only, dark. tsc clean; unit+integration+e2e 12/12 green; npm run lint clean.

CORRECTNESS FIX (follow-up, 2026-07-22): the original approach resolved each live session's login slot from its recorded subscriptionAccountId via a pool lookup — WRONG for the feature's motivating case. Under identity drift the recorded subscriptionAccountId DIVERGES from the config home the session actually runs on (verified live: session echo-llm-pathway-characterization recorded subscriptionAccountId=adriana while its real CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR was the login-missing justin-gmail slot), so the account lookup landed on the wrong slot and the guard silently never fired for exactly the case it exists for. FIX: correlate on the session's REAL config home (its live CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR). SessionManager had no native config-home accessor (the Session record has no configHome field), so added SessionManager.configHomeForSession(tmuxSession) mirroring the existing getSessionFramework tmux-env read (withSyncOp-funneled execFileSync 'show-environment CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR', 2s timeout, fail-toward-silence -> undefined -> session skipped, never guessed; not cached since a swap/respawn can re-point a live name). RunningSessionView now carries configHome (not subscriptionAccountId); getLiveSessions correlates on it directly; server wiring populates it via configHomeForSession. Factory stays pure/unit-testable on injected configHome data — the tmux read lives natively in SessionManager. Unit test adds the drift-scenario case (recorded account adriana, real config home = login-missing justin-gmail -> correlates on real home -> stranded on justin-gmail) plus the inverse. tsc 0; unit+integration+e2e 14/14 green; npm run lint clean (sync-subprocess-chokepoint / guard-manifest / dev-agent-dark-gate all clean).
